note: Official primary sources only — for human verification. No vendor/blog sources.

sources.name: EUR-Lex — EU Law Portal
sources.scope: Authoritative full text of all EU legislation, directives, regulations and Official Journal publications

sources.name: European Commission — CE Marking
sources.scope: Official EC guidance on CE marking obligations, scope, and procedures for manufacturers/importers

sources.name: European Commission — Harmonised Standards (single-market portal)
sources.scope: Index page linking to per-directive harmonised-standard lists published in the Official Journal

sources.name: European Commission — Harmonised Standards for RED 2014/53/EU
sources.scope: Current list of harmonised standards under the Radio Equipment Directive (RED) 2014/53/EU

sources.name: Directive 2014/35/EU — Low Voltage Directive (LVD)
sources.scope: LVD: safety requirements for electrical equipment rated 50–1000 V AC or 75–1500 V DC, including PV inverters

sources.name: Commission Implementing Decision (EU) 2023/2723 — Harmonised Standards List for LVD 2014/35/EU
sources.scope: Latest Official Journal list of harmonised standards conferring presumption of conformity under LVD

sources.name: Directive 2014/30/EU — EMC Directive
sources.scope: EMC Directive: electromagnetic compatibility requirements for all electrical/electronic apparatus

sources.name: Commission Implementing Decision (EU) 2019/1326 — Harmonised Standards List for EMC 2014/30/EU
sources.scope: Consolidated Official Journal list of harmonised standards conferring presumption of conformity under EMC Directive

sources.name: Directive 2014/53/EU — Radio Equipment Directive (RED)
sources.scope: RED: radio equipment placing-on-market requirements; cyber Articles 3(3)(d-f) activated by Delegated Reg 2022/30

sources.name: Commission Delegated Regulation (EU) 2022/30 — RED Cyber Articles 3(3)(d-f)
sources.scope: Activates RED cybersecurity essential requirements (network resilience, privacy, fraud) for internet-connected radio equipment; applies from 1 Aug 2025; repealed by CRA from 11 Dec 2027

sources.name: Commission Implementing Decision (EU) 2022/2191 — Harmonised Standards List for RED 2014/53/EU
sources.scope: Official Journal list of harmonised standards under RED including EN 18031 series (cybersecurity); published OJ L 289, 10 Nov 2022

sources.name: NANDO — New Approach Notified and Designated Organisations
sources.scope: European Commission database of all notified bodies designated under EU harmonisation directives and regulations

sources.name: Regulation (EU) 2019/1020 — Market Surveillance and Compliance of Products
sources.scope: EU market surveillance framework: obligations on economic operators, border controls, RAPEX, applies from 16 Jul 2021

sources.name: Commission Regulation (EU) 2016/631 — Network Code on Requirements for Generators (RfG)
sources.scope: Binding EU grid-connection requirements for all generator types (Type A–D) including PV inverters; basis for national grid codes

sources.name: ENTSO-E — Network Code RfG (Requirements for Generators)
sources.scope: ENTSO-E official RfG page: implementation guidance, FAQs, and links to national implementation reports

sources.name: ACER — EU Agency for the Cooperation of Energy Regulators
sources.scope: ACER: oversight of EU network codes implementation, opinions and recommendations on grid codes and energy regulation

sources.name: CEN-CENELEC Standards Catalogue (standards.cencenelec.eu)
sources.scope: Official searchable catalogue of all CEN and CENELEC European Standards (EN), including EN 50549 grid-connection series

sources.name: CEN-CENELEC — Obtaining European Standards
sources.scope: Official guidance on how EN standards (including EN 50549-1/-2 grid-connection standards) are obtained via national members

sources.name: VDE FNN — Technical Connection Rules (VDE-AR-N 4105 & 4110)
sources.scope: Germany: VDE-AR-N 4110 (MV, ≤36 kV) and VDE-AR-N 4105 (LV) — technical connection rules for generators including PV inverters

sources.name: VDE FNN — Documents (publications list)
sources.scope: Germany: index of all VDE FNN technical connection rule publications and application notes

sources.name: ENA — Engineering Recommendation G98 & G99
sources.scope: UK: G98 (≤16 A/phase LV) and G99 (>16 A/phase or MV/HV) — grid connection and protection requirements for distributed generation

sources.name: ENA — All G98 & G99 Forms
sources.scope: UK: official application forms and amendment history for G98 and G99 (Issue 2 current as of March 2025)

sources.name: CEI — Standards CEI 0-16 and CEI 0-21 (Comitato Elettrotecnico Italiano)
sources.scope: Italy: CEI 0-21 (LV grid connection for generating plants) and CEI 0-16 (MV/HV active-user connection); aligned with EU 2016/631

sources.name: Directive 2011/65/EU — RoHS 2 (Restriction of Hazardous Substances)
sources.scope: RoHS: restricts Pb, Hg, Cd, Cr(VI), PBB, PBDE (and 4 phthalates from 2019) in EEE including power electronics

sources.name: Directive 2012/19/EU — WEEE (Waste Electrical and Electronic Equipment)
sources.scope: WEEE: producer take-back, collection, and recycling obligations for EEE including inverters

sources.name: Regulation (EC) 1907/2006 — REACH
sources.scope: REACH: registration, evaluation, authorisation and restriction of chemicals (SVHCs) in articles including EEE

sources.name: ECHA — REACH Substances Restricted and SVHCs
sources.scope: ECHA authoritative list of REACH Annex XVII restricted substances and Annex XIV SVHC candidate list

sources.name: Regulation (EU) 2024/2847 — Cyber Resilience Act (CRA)
sources.scope: CRA: horizontal cybersecurity requirements for products with digital elements; most provisions apply from 11 Dec 2027

sources.name: Directive (EU) 2022/2555 — NIS2 (Network and Information Systems Security)
sources.scope: NIS2: cybersecurity risk management and incident reporting for operators of essential/important services; applied from 18 Oct 2024

sources.name: ENISA — NIS2 Directive Hub
sources.scope: ENISA guidance, technical implementation documents and EU vulnerability registry for NIS2 compliance

sources.name: IEC Webstore — International Electrotechnical Commission
sources.scope: Authoritative catalogue and purchase portal for all IEC standards including IEC 62109 (PV inverter safety) and IEC 61000 (EMC) series

sources.name: IEC 62109-2:2011 — Safety of power converters for PV systems, Part 2: Inverters
sources.scope: IEC 62109-2: particular safety requirements for DC–AC inverters in PV power systems; basis for CE/CCC inverter safety testing

sources.name: IEC 62109-3:2020 — Safety of power converters for PV systems, Part 3: Electronic devices incorporated in PV modules
sources.scope: IEC 62109-3: safety requirements for electronics integrated with PV modules or DC-optimiser systems

sources.name: IEC 61000 Series — Electromagnetic Compatibility (IEC Webstore)
sources.scope: IEC 61000 EMC family: immunity and emission limits used globally; underpins EN 61000/EN IEC 61000 harmonised standards in EU

sources.name: IECEE — CB Scheme (IEC System for Conformity Assessment)
sources.scope: IECEE CB Scheme: mutual recognition of test reports and certificates among 52 member bodies for EEE including PV inverters

sources.name: SAMR — National Standard Full-Text Open System (openstd.samr.gov.cn)
sources.scope: China: authoritative full-text free access to mandatory GB and recommended GB/T national standards; managed by SAMR/SAC

sources.name: SAMR — National Standard Information Public Service Platform (std.samr.gov.cn)
sources.scope: China: bibliographic search across all national, industry and local standards; English interface available; managed by SAMR/SAC

sources.name: CNCA — Certification and Accreditation Administration of China (English portal)
sources.scope: CNCA: administers China Compulsory Certification (CCC/3C) scheme; official English portal for policy, scope, and implementing institutions

sources.name: CNCA — CCC Certificate Query System (cx.cnca.cn)
sources.scope: China: authoritative online database to verify authenticity and validity of CCC certificates by certificate number or product model

sources.name: MOFCOM — TBT/SPS Enquiry Point (chinawto.mofcom.gov.cn)
sources.scope: China: Ministry of Commerce TBT and SPS notification and enquiry point; access to China's WTO TBT notifications and trade-barrier investigation procedures
